ew_context: Price appears to be completing a corrective wave 2 or wave B within the falling wedge, setting up for impulsive move higher toward prior highs
pattern_desc: Falling wedge pattern with converging support at 88.66 and resistance line connecting highs at 117.63, 110.93, and 109.47. Bullish divergence setup near apex.
key_levels: Support: 88.66 (wedge support line). Resistance: 109.47 (recent high), 117.63 (wedge target)
invalidation: Close below 88.66 on D1 timeframe would break wedge support and negate the bullish reversal pattern
market_context: Oil markets supported by Middle East tensions and US strikes despite peace deal hopes, with supply disruption concerns offsetting demand fears
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

Oil has formed a falling wedge pattern with clear converging trendlines, currently testing support near 88.66 after the recent decline from 117.63. The pattern typically resolves with an upside breakout targeting the prior high at 117.63. Price action shows classic wedge characteristics with decreasing volatility and volume into the apex.

RSI at 38.1% indicates oversold conditions ripe for reversal, while EMA20 at 99.26 above EMA50 at 95.04 maintains the underlying bullish trend structure. ATR at 6.18 justifies the 13.37-point stop loss placement below key wedge support.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 109.47 (recent swing high and breakout confirmation level)
- **Support**: 88.66 (critical wedge support line)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 88.66 on D1 timeframe would break the wedge support trendline and negate the bullish reversal setup.

## Macro & Flow Context

Middle East tensions from US strikes support oil prices despite peace deal uncertainty. CFD flow shows laminar congestion but momentum misalignment suggests potential for explosive move higher on breakout.
